page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8163 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, providing 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core running at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, alongside support for AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI instruction sets.

The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and data hosting. Network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance type.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average to weak tiers depending on the workload. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 328.71 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 40.9 GB/sec. However, raw compute benchmarks such as stress-ng single-core and multi-core tests register in the bottom 25 percent, and Geekbench scores reflect similar weak performance. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput perform in the middle 50 percent tier, while LLM inference speeds are average for small 135M models but drop to poor for larger 7B models.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.g5.2xlarge offers a stable resource profile for general-purpose workloads. It is best suited for applications requiring consistent CPU performance without extreme compute demands, such as moderate web serving, Redis caching, and basic application hosting. The lack of local storage and lower relative compute benchmarks suggest it is less optimal for high-performance computing or storage-heavy database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average memory bandwidth and static web serving capabilities, though raw single-core and multi-core compute metrics fall into the lower performance tiers. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as moderate web serving, caching, and standard application hosting that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand high-performance computing or local storage.

tagline: A dedicated general-purpose virtual machine offering a balanced memory-to-core ratio for stable, moderate-scale application hosting and caching workloads.
